title: Screenshots Data
description: Schema for the screenshots.json data file that tracks static screenshots and animated GIFs used in the README and docs.

version.description: Version of the project this data applies to (semver string).

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 date when this file was last updated (YYYY-MM-DD).

Screenshot.title: Screenshot
Screenshot.description: A static screenshot asset referenced from the README or docs.

file.description: Relative path to the image file from the repository root.

alt.description: Alt text for the image (used in Markdown and for accessibility).

width.description: Native pixel width of the image.

height.description: Native pixel height of the image.

category.description: Logical grouping used to filter screenshots (e.g. 'readme', 'features', 'cli').

page.description: The web route or context where this screenshot was captured (e.g. '/collection', 'modal', 'terminal').

features.description: List of feature IDs (from features.json) that this screenshot illustrates.

captured.description: ISO 8601 date when this screenshot was last captured, or null if not yet captured.

notes.description: Internal notes for the screenshot author (viewport state, required data, etc.).

Gif.title: Animated GIF
Gif.description: An animated GIF asset demonstrating a workflow or interaction.

file.description: Relative path to the GIF file from the repository root.

duration.description: Approximate playback duration as a human-readable range (e.g. '20-30s').

tool.description: Recording tool used to produce the GIF (e.g. 'asciinema', 'claude-in-chrome').

placeholder_note.description: Explanation for why this GIF is still a placeholder.

sequence.description: Ordered list of steps that make up the recorded workflow. Items may be plain strings (legacy) or structured step objects.

labels.description: High-level phase labels shown as on-screen overlays during playback.

GifStep.title: GIF Sequence Step
GifStep.description: A structured step in a GIF recording sequence.

url.description: URL or route to navigate to (used when action is 'navigate').

target.description: CSS selector or accessible label of the element to interact with.

label.description: On-screen caption shown as an overlay during this step.

hold.description: Duration in seconds to pause on this step before continuing.
